meaning: n.<美>(载货火车最后一节的)守车,<英>轮船上的厨房
examples: The train. The caboose is just a part.
The locomotive is not the same as the caboose.
A caboose isn't the same thing as a locomotive.
No election campaign during the 1800s would be complete without a "whistle-stop tour" - when candidates would charter trains and speak to the voters from the caboose.
Still, a universal format for transmitting 1080p content around the home is certainly something that's easy to dig -- it's getting everyone on a single caboose that's difficult.
